model.name: Llama 3.1 405B Instruct official mixed FP8/BF16 weight + BF16 KV deployment

metadata.architecture_summary: Dense causal decoder-only Transformer with 126 SwiGLU layers, 128K full-context GQA, Llama 3 RoPE scaling, pre-RMSNorm, and untied input embedding and LM head. The official production checkpoint uses FP8 only for the middle 124 layers' FFN projections; attention and the remaining trainable tensors stay BF16.

weight_capacity.capacity_note: Capacity contains all trainable tensors, including both untied embedding/head matrices and RMSNorm, plus the official checkpoint's FP32 per-output-row FFN weight scales. It excludes tokenizer files and serialization/container overhead.

metadata.parameter_count_convention: The engine counts parameterized matrices executed for one Decode token: all Q/K/V/O and SwiGLU projections plus the complete untied LM head. The separate input embedding is resident capacity but Decode reads only one 16384-element BF16 row per request. RMSNorm, RoPE, activation quantization/dequantization, softmax, and other elementwise operations do not enter matrix-parameter FLOPs.

precision_profile.weight_precision_evidence: The technical report and official Hugging Face release describe W8A8 dynamic row-wise FP8 for gate/up/down projections in most middle-layer FFNs, while self-attention and the first/last Transformer layers remain BF16. Official checkpoint tensor totals independently reproduce that split.
precision_profile.kv_precision_evidence: The official reference implementation allocates KV cache using the projected query/key execution dtype. Attention remains on the unquantized BF16 path in this checkpoint, so this configuration records persistent KV as BF16. This is implementation-derived evidence rather than an explicit model-card KV declaration.
precision_profile.quantization_metadata_capacity: The 15237120 FP32 static weight-scale elements are included separately in total_weight_capacity_bytes.
precision_profile.quantization_metadata_traffic: The current schema has no batch-shared non-compute weight-metadata traffic field. Adding scales as a parameter group would falsely add FLOPs, while fixed_cost would use the wrong per-request normalization. Engine weight_read_bytes therefore contains matrix payload only; the exact scale bytes are retained above for later schema extension.
precision_profile.runtime_quantization_note: Dynamic activation scales, activation quantization/dequantization operations, kernel padding, and temporary buffers are outside the current static logical-HBM workload boundary.

metadata.context_note: 131072 is the native Llama 3.1 release context. The configuration models full attention at every selected C; it does not treat 128K as a synthetic extrapolation.
metadata.checkpoint_revision_note: The initial FP8 repository state duplicated 16 KV heads from tensor-parallel shards. The pinned 2024-08-16 correction records the actual architecture's 8 KV heads; using the initial index would double KV projection and cache counts.
metadata.embedding_note: Input embedding and LM head are not tied. Capacity includes both full BF16 matrices. Decode reads one 32768-byte BF16 embedding row per request, while the full BF16 LM head is read once per scheduler step and amortized across the batch.
metadata.implementation_note: The logical-superchip baseline excludes physical tensor/pipeline-parallel communication, collective traffic, kernel padding, and temporary activation materialization.
